volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Oedipus at Colonus (Section 115 - Section 221)

sections.label: Section 115
sections.original: τίνας λόγους ἐροῦσιν · ἐν γὰρ τῷ μαθεῖν
sections.translation: how these men will speak . For in learning is the safeguard of our course . They exit .

sections.label: Section 1.118
sections.original: ποῦ κυρεῖ ἐκτόπιος συθεὶς ὁ πάντων
sections.translation: Look ! Who was he , then ? Where is he staying ? Where has he rushed from this place ,

sections.label: Section 1.120
sections.translation: man most insatiate of all who live ? Scan the ground , look well , press the search everywhere . A wanderer that old man must have been ,

sections.label: Section 1.125
sections.translation: a wanderer , not a dweller in the land ; otherwise he never would have advanced into this untrodden grove of the maidens with whom none may strive .

sections.label: Section 1.130
sections.translation:
Their name we tremble to speak ; we pass them by with eyes turned away , moving our lips , without sound or word , in still devotion .
But now it is said that one has come who reveres them not at all ;

sections.label: Section 1.135
sections.original: ὃν ἐγὼ λεύσσων περὶ πᾶν οὔπω
sections.translation: and him I cannot yet discern , though I look round all the holy place , nor do I know where to find his lodging .

sections.label: Section 1.138
sections.original: ὅδʼ ἐκεῖνος ἐγώ · φωνῇ γὰρ ὁρῶ ,
sections.translation: Behold the man you seek ! In sound is my sight , as the saying goes .

sections.label: Section 1.140
sections.translation: Oh ! Oh ! Fearful he is to see , and fearful to hear !

sections.label: Section 1.142
sections.translation: Do not regard me , I beg you , as a lawless man .

sections.label: Section 1.143
sections.original: Ζεῦ ἀλεξῆτορ , τίς ποθʼ ὁ πρέσβυς ;
sections.translation: Zeus defend us ! Who may this old man be ?

sections.label: Section 1.144
sections.translation: Not so wholly of the best fate

sections.label: Section 1.145
sections.translation: that you would call him fortunate , guardians of this land ! It is plain ; otherwise I would not be creeping , as you see , by the eyes of others , and buoying my strength upon weakness .

sections.label: Section 1.148
sections.translation: Alas ! Were you sightless even from birth ?

sections.label: Section 1.150
sections.translation: Evil have been your days , and many , it appears . But at least if I can help it , you shall not add this curse to your lot . You go too far — too far !

sections.label: Section 1.155
sections.original: περᾷς γάρ , περᾷς · ἀλλʼ ἵνα τῷδʼ ἐν ἀ-
sections.translation: That your rash steps may intrude on the field of this voiceless , grassy glade , where the waters of the mixing bowl blend their stream with the flow of honied offerings , beware , unhappiest of strangers .

sections.label: Section 1.160
sections.translation: Retire ! Withdraw ! Let a wide space part us . Do you hear , toil-worn wanderer ? If you have anything to say in converse with us ,

sections.label: Section 1.165
sections.translation: leave forbidden ground , and speak where it is lawful for all ; but , till then , refrain .

sections.label: Section 1.170
sections.translation: Daughter , to what counsel shall we incline ?

sections.label: Section 1.171
sections.original: ὦ πάτερ , ἀστοῖς ἴσα χρὴ μελετᾶν ,
sections.translation: My father , we must behave just as the townspeople do , listening and giving way where it is necessary .

sections.label: Section 1.173
sections.translation: Then give me your hand .

sections.label: Section 1.173a
sections.translation: I lay it in yours .

sections.label: Section 1.174
sections.original: ὦ ξένε , μὴ δῆτʼ ἀδικηθῶ σοὶ
sections.translation: Strangers , let me not suffer wrong

sections.label: Section 1.175
sections.translation: when I have trusted in you , and have passed from my refuge !

sections.label: Section 2.176
sections.original: οὔ τοι μήποτέ σʼ ἐκ τῶνδʼ ἑδράνων ,
sections.translation: Never , old man , never will anyone remove you from your resting-place here against your will .

sections.label: Section 2.178
sections.translation: Further , then ?

sections.label: Section 2.178a
sections.translation: Come still further .

sections.label: Section 2.180
sections.translation: Further ?

sections.label: Section 2.181c
sections.translation: Come , follow this way with your dark steps , father , as I lead you .

sections.label: Section 2.184
sections.translation: Stranger in a foreign land ,

sections.label: Section 2.185
sections.original: ὦ τλάμων , ὅ τι καὶ πόλις
sections.translation: poor man , have the courage to detest what the city steadfastly holds as not dear , and to reverence what it holds dear !

sections.label: Section 2.188
sections.translation: Lead me , then , child , to a spot where I may speak and listen within piety ’ s domain ,

sections.label: Section 2.190
sections.original: τὸ μὲν εἴποιμεν , τὸ δʼ ἀκούσαιμεν ,
sections.translation: and let us not wage war with necessity .

sections.label: Section 2.192
sections.original: αὐτοῦ · μηκέτι τοῦδʼ αὐτοπέτρου βήματος ἔξω πόδα κλίνῃς .
sections.translation: There ! Do not incline your steps beyond that ledge of bedrock .

sections.label: Section 2.193
sections.translation: This far ?

sections.label: Section 2.193a
sections.translation: Enough , I say

sections.label: Section 2.195
sections.translation: Shall I sit down ?

sections.label: Section 2.195a
sections.translation: Yes , move sideways , and crouch low on the edge of the rock .

sections.label: Section 2.198
sections.translation: Father , this is my task : calmly to —

sections.label: Section 2.199
sections.translation: Ah me ! ah me !

sections.label: Section 2.200
sections.translation: — fit step to step , and lean your aged frame upon my dear arm .

sections.label: Section 2.202
sections.translation: Alas for my sad destruction !

sections.label: Section 2.203
sections.translation: Ah , poor man , since now you are at ease , speak ! What is your lineage among mortals ? With what name are you led on your weary path ?

sections.label: Section 2.205
sections.original: τίς ὁ πολύπονος ἄγει ; τίνʼ ἂν
sections.translation: What fatherland can you tell us of ?

sections.label: Section 207
sections.translation: Strangers , I am without a city , but do not —

sections.label: Section 208
sections.translation: What is this that you forbid , old man ?

sections.label: Section 210
sections.original: μὴ μὴ μή μʼ ἀνέρῃ τίς εἰμι ,
sections.translation: do not , do not ask me who I am ! Do not seek or probe further !

sections.label: Section 211
sections.translation: What does this mean ?

sections.label: Section 212
sections.translation: Horrid the birth —

sections.label: Section 213
sections.translation: Speak !

sections.label: Section 214
sections.original: τίνος εἶ σπέρματος , ὦ ξένε , φώνει , πατρόθεν .
sections.translation: My child — ah , me !— what shall I say ?

sections.label: Section 215
sections.original: ὤμοι ἐγώ , τί πάθω , τέκνον ἐμόν ;
sections.translation: What is your lineage , stranger ? Speak ! And who is your father ?

sections.label: Section 216
sections.translation: Woe is me ! What will become of me , my child ?

sections.label: Section 217
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐρῶ · οὐ γὰρ ἔχω κατακρυφάν .
sections.translation: Speak , for you are driven to the verge .

sections.label: Section 218
sections.translation: Then speak I will . I have no way to hide it .

sections.label: Section 220
sections.translation: Do you know of a son of Laius ?

sections.label: Section 220a
sections.translation: Oh !

sections.label: Section 221
sections.translation: — And the race of the Labdacidae ?
